notes.note: The relayer of a third-hand rumour: Hermogenes Clodius reported that Andromenes had seen Cicero's son. The double name marks him as a freedman or client of a Clodius.
The original source of the report that Cicero's son had been sighted at Corcyra, en route to or from his studies; otherwise unknown.

notes.anchor: he had seen Cicero at Corcyra
notes.note: 'Cicero' here is the orator's son, Marcus the younger, then travelling to study in Athens. Corcyra (Corfu) lay on the sea route from Italy to Greece.
The diphtherai (Greek for prepared animal skins), here the four books of the Academica being made ready for Varro. Cicero leaves to Atticus whether to present them.
The retentio of letter 13.23: a debtor's clause holding back part of a payment. Cicero urges Atticus to close the account at once, without hesitation or reconsideration.
